data.title: Commerzbank: Japan's upcoming election could lead to a depreciation of the yen
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News Volkmar Baur, an analyst at Commerzbank, said in a report that Japan's upcoming elections could be a turning point for the country and have an adverse impact on the yen. Polls show that the government is in danger of losing its majority in Sunday's Senate elections. He also said that the possibility of holding new elections in the House of Representatives cannot be ruled out. This increases uncertainty about future fiscal policy and makes trade negotiations with the United States more difficult, "which will definitely weaken the yen." Baur said that even if there are no changes, the yen may fall if the government launches a new fiscal plan in response to the election results but fails to address structural problems. (Jinshi)

data.title: Singapore's Basel Medical Halts Bitcoin Acquisition Strategy Amid U.S. Regulatory Uncertainty
data.description: According to PANews, Singapore-based orthopedic medical group Basel Medical, listed on Nasdaq under the ticker BMGL, has announced a suspension of its Bitcoin acquisition strategy. The decision comes in response to ongoing uncertainties surrounding U.S. regulations on digital assets. The company cited the lack of clarity in the regulatory environment as a key factor influencing its decision to pause further investments in Bitcoin.

data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Fed Governor Waller: If the Fed waits another six weeks to cut interest rates, it may not be a big problem, but there is no reason to continue delaying. (Jinshi)

data.title: The chairman of the US SEC said that the agency is considering introducing innovative exemption policies to encourage the development of asset tokenization
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News After the U.S. House of Representatives passed a landmark stablecoin bill early Thursday, U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) Chairman Paul Atkins said the SEC is considering an innovation exemption policy to encourage the development of asset tokenization. He said at a press conference: "SEC staff are considering what other changes need to be made within our regulatory framework to encourage asset tokenization, including the establishment of an innovation exemption policy to allow novel trading methods and the introduction of more precise exemption measures to promote the construction of other components of the tokenized securities ecosystem." Atkins praised the passage of stablecoin legislation and told reporters that the SEC looks forward to "establishing clear rules" for the digital asset field. The bill has now been submitted to President Trump for signature. (Bloomberg)

data.title: Bitcoin Self-Custody Growth Declines Following Spot ETF Launch
data.description: According to Foresight News, cryptocurrency analyst Willy Woo has noted a decline in the growth rate of Bitcoin self-custody users since the introduction of spot ETFs. This marks a break in the 15-year trend of increasing self-custody adoption among Bitcoin users.

data.title: Turbos DEX and Turbos.Fun completed the upgrade, comprehensively improving the Sui chain transaction and interactive experience
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News Turbos Finance announced the completion of the mobile upgrade of Turbos DEX and Turbos.Fun.
TurbosDEX partially optimized the dashboard navigation, users can switch between Swap and Pools pages with one click; token selection is smarter, highlighting hot trending assets; LP management is more efficient, the deposit and withdrawal interface is moved up and APRs and fees are clearly displayed.
Turbos.Fun launched a smart navigation bar, which supports one-click access to token creation, rankings and increase lists, and adds a "Trade" button on each Meme coin page to facilitate quick transactions and view exchange information.

data.title: Hedge Fund Achieves Remarkable Returns in Cryptocurrency Sector
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, a key strategist behind the lobbying efforts that turned U.S. President Donald Trump into a strong supporter of cryptocurrency is making significant investments in one of the industry's hottest areas, yielding impressive returns.

Insiders report that David Bailey's hedge fund, 210k Capital, achieved a net return of 640% over the 12 months ending in June, far surpassing Bitcoin's own growth. The fund has invested in approximately a dozen companies that have transitioned into Bitcoin purchasers.

As of June 30, 210k Capital managed assets totaling $433 million.

data.title: dYdX acquires crypto social trading platform Pocket Protector, marking its first external acquisition
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News Decentralized derivatives exchange dYdX has completed the acquisition of crypto social trading platform Pocket Protector, which is its first external acquisition. The transaction was completed in the form of a combination of cash + DYDX tokens, and the specific amount was not disclosed. The two co-founders of Pocket Protector will join dYdX as president and head of growth respectively, and the Telegram trading robot (including copy trading function) and the 9-person technical team they developed will be incorporated into dYdX. Pocket Protector was founded last year and has raised $7 million from investors such as Electric Capital and Dragonfly. (The Block)

data.title: Bit Digital increased its holdings by 19,683 ETH, bringing its total holdings to over 120,000 ETH
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News Nasdaq-listed Bit Digital announced that it has used the net proceeds from the recently completed $67.3 million registered private placement to institutional investors to purchase approximately 19,683 Ethereum. After the transaction is completed, Bit Digital holds approximately 120,306 ETH. (PRNewswire)

data.title: U.S. SEC Chair Highlights Regulatory Focus on Cryptocurrency
data.description: According to PANews, U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission Chair Atkins emphasized the importance of establishing clear regulatory guidelines for cryptocurrencies. He noted that stablecoins fall under the jurisdiction of banking regulatory bodies.
